projectDescription: A Python DSL and MLIR-based compiler stack for authoring high-performance GPU kernels with explicit layout algebra and tiling on AMD ROCm hardware.
keyFeatures: Python DSL (@flyc.kernel / @flyc.jit) for writing GPU kernels with layout algebra
Explicit tiling hierarchy across block, warp, thread, and instruction scopes
MLIR-native Fly dialect with layout IR, coordinate mapping, and composable lowering pipeline
Embedded MLIR Python runtime — no external mlir wheel required
JIT compilation with disk caching to AMD GPU binaries (ROCDL/fatbin)
Production kernels: GEMM (preshuffle, blockscale, MoE), FlashAttention, LayerNorm, RMSNorm, Softmax, PagedAttention
Triton-style autotuner (@autotune decorator) for searching tile configurations
Support for AMD CDNA3/CDNA4 (MI300X/MI350/MI355X) and RDNA4/gfx1250 (MI450)
publicApiSurface: @flyc.kernel — decorate a Python function as a GPU kernel
@flyc.jit — JIT-compile and launch a host-side function
flydsl.expr (fx) — layout, copy, arithmetic, vector, GPU, ROCDL ops
fx.crd2idx / fx.idx2crd — coordinate ↔ linear index mapping
fx.make_tiled_copy / fx.make_copy_atom / fx.copy_atom_call — tiled copy API
fx.Tensor / fx.Stream / fx.Constexpr / fx.Int32 — argument types
kernels.preshuffle_gemm / kernels.layernorm_kernel / kernels.softmax_kernel / kernels.pa_decode_fp8 — pre-built kernels
